verdict.answer_first: B states the actionable answer in sentence 1: "default I'd run with" and immediately prescribes "TTL-based cache-aside with Redis"; A buries the plan behind "decision path" and starts with measurement.
verdict.decisiveness: B commits to one default: "TTL-based cache-aside with Redis" and says when to upgrade; A surveys many options: "In-process", "Shared cache", "TTL only", "Write-through".
verdict.fabrication: none; both use conditional/general thresholds, but A's "if 80% of read load is 10–20 distinct queries" and "~70–80%" are heuristic, not claimed facts from the prompt.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to execute: "Set TTLs of 30–60s... add TTL jitter + a per-key lock... ship one query behind a flag first"; A is more complete but slower to convert into first action.
